info.title: Shipwright Task Store API
info.description: REST API for the Shipwright task-store service — task queue management, PR tracking, and scoped token administration.

200.description: List of tasks with total count

get.summary: Get distinct session and repo values

post.summary: Touch heartbeatAt on a claimed task
Release a task back to pending
Create a new token — raw value returned exactly once

201.description: Created token including the one-time raw value

schema.description: When true, return only unclaimed PRs (claimedBy IS NULL) — mirrors /tasks?ready=true. Composable with other filters (repo, state, reviewState); does not itself apply state/reviewState eligibility rules the way claim-next does.

409.description: Conflict — already claimed with same commitSha

post.summary: Atomic find-and-claim of oldest eligible PR
Touch heartbeatAt for a claimed PR
Increment patchCycles and conditionally reset reviewState=pending

skipped.description: IDs of tasks skipped because they already exist (Prisma P2002 unique constraint collision).

prCreatedAt.description: ISO timestamp of the GitHub PR's actual creation time. Set once via POST /prs/claim (first claim only); read-only thereafter.

claimedBy.description: Agent claiming this PR (admin tokens only)

phase.description: Pipeline phase this claim is for (defaults to 'review' when omitted)

prCreatedAt.description: ISO timestamp of the GitHub PR's actual creation time. Only applied on first claim (record creation); ignored on subsequent claims since the field is immutable once set.

agentId.description: Agent ID (admin tokens only; agent tokens use token identity)

commitSha.description: Current head commit SHA. When provided and it differs from the record's stored commitSha, reviewState resets to pending and commitSha is updated. When it matches, reviewState is left untouched (no-op patch cycle). When omitted, reviewState unconditionally resets to pending (legacy behavior).
